hypotheses.statement: Increasing temperature from 20°C to 40°C will cause a proportional increase in the reaction rate of the enzyme catalase, as measured by the volume of oxygen gas produced from hydrogen peroxide decompo

For the enzyme amylase, a 10°C increase in temperature within the range of 25°C to 55°C will cause a decrease in the Michaelis constant (Km) by at least 20%, indicating increased substrate binding aff

Exposure of the enzyme lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) to a sustained temperature of 45°C for 30 minutes will cause an irreversible reduction in its specific activity by over 50% compared to a control kep

novelty_checker_note: NoveltyChecker exists but never called from HypothesisGeneratorAgent
